name: the primitive type called oak

VINIT.justification: The state of zero topological charge before any symmetry is imposed.

TANCH.justification: The global constraint that defines the limits of the symmetry-protected phase.

AFWD.justification: The transformation from a trivial vacuum to a state with defined Z2 parity.

AREV.justification: The reverse operation that flips the sign of the topological sector.

CLINK.justification: The sequential application of symmetry operations within the Z2 group.

IMSCRIB.justification: The self-recognition of the state under the trivial group element.

FSPLIT.justification: The division of the system into even and odd topological sectors.

FFUSE.justification: The merging of parity sectors to recover the total Hilbert space.

EVALT.justification: The affirmative state where the Z2 eigenvalue is +1.

EVALF.justification: The negative state where the Z2 eigenvalue is -1.

ENGAGR.justification: A state existing in both parity sectors simultaneously before measurement.

IFIX.justification: The permanent recording of the winding number in the system memory.

steps.domain_action: initialize the field in a trivial vacuum state
identify the field as a candidate for topological protection
apply Z2 symmetry constraints to the field
branch the field into distinct parity sectors
validate the even-parity sector as the primary vacuum
compose the even-parity state with a local perturbation
record the local topological charge in the invariant log
apply a parity flip to the secondary branch
validate the odd-parity sector as a topological excitation
record the excitation charge in the invariant log
recombine the sectors into a unified protected manifold
anchor the manifold within the Z2 invariant boundary

phase_6.delta_s_verdict: ΔS ≈ 0 — the protection is topologically robust and dissipationless
